title: Introduction
content:
## Purpose and Scope

This Digital Adaptation Kit (DAK) provides comprehensive guidance for the implementation of the Core Information Standard version 2.0 within digital health systems. The Core Information Standard represents a foundational framework for capturing, storing, and exchanging essential patient information across health and care settings in the United Kingdom. This DAK translates the clinical and informational requirements of the standard into actionable technical specifications suitable for digital implementation.

The scope of this document encompasses the complete lifecycle of patient information management, from initial demographic data capture through to complex care planning and end-of-life considerations. It addresses the needs of diverse healthcare providers including general practitioners, specialists, nurses, social workers, and allied health professionals working across primary, secondary, and community care settings.

## Background and Context

The UK healthcare system faces increasing pressure to deliver integrated, patient-centered care across multiple providers and settings. Fragmented information systems have historically created barriers to effective care coordination, leading to duplicated efforts, missed opportunities for intervention, and potential patient safety risks. The NHS Long Term Plan emphasizes the critical importance of interoperable digital systems that support seamless information sharing while maintaining patient privacy and security.

The Core Information Standard emerged from extensive consultation with healthcare professionals, patients, and technical experts to identify the essential data elements required for safe and effective care delivery. It builds upon existing NHS data standards while introducing structured approaches to capturing patient preferences, social circumstances, and care planning information that have traditionally been poorly documented in clinical systems.

## Relationship to Clinical Guidelines

This DAK supports the implementation of various NICE guidelines and NHS England policies by ensuring that critical patient information is available at the point of care. It particularly aligns with:

- NICE guideline NG56 on multimorbidity, which emphasizes the importance of comprehensive assessment and personalized care planning
- NHS England's Universal Personalized Care model, requiring systematic capture of patient goals and preferences
- The Mental Capacity Act 2005 requirements for documenting capacity assessments and best interest decisions
- Equality Act 2010 obligations for recording and acting upon reasonable adjustments

The standard does not prescribe clinical pathways but rather ensures that the information needed to follow evidence-based guidelines is consistently available across care settings.

## Document Overview

This DAK is structured according to WHO SMART Guidelines methodology, providing:

- **Health Interventions & Recommendations**: Key clinical and care activities supported by the standard
- **Generic Personas**: Representative users who will interact with systems implementing the standard
- **User Scenarios**: Typical workflows demonstrating how the standard supports care delivery
- **Business Processes**: Detailed process maps showing information flow and decision points
- **Core Data Elements**: Comprehensive data dictionary with validation rules and terminology bindings
- **Decision Support Logic**: Automated rules for data quality, clinical safety alerts, and care coordination
- **Indicators & Metrics**: Measurable outcomes for monitoring implementation success
- **Requirements**: Functional and non-functional specifications for system implementation

Each section builds upon the previous, creating a complete picture of how the Core Information Standard transforms from policy intent to operational reality within digital health systems.

title: Health Interventions & Recommendations
content:
## Summary of Key Clinical Recommendations

The Core Information Standard supports a wide range of health interventions by ensuring that essential patient information is available for clinical decision-making. The following interventions are enabled or enhanced through proper implementation of this standard:

## Table of Interventions

| Intervention | Recommendation | Source/Guideline | Evidence Grade |
|---|---|---|---|
| Comprehensive Patient Assessment | All patients should have demographic details, contact information, and GP registration recorded at first contact | NHS Data Security Standards, NICE NG56 | A (Mandatory requirement) |
| Reasonable Adjustments Identification | Healthcare providers must identify and document required adjustments for patients with disabilities | Equality Act 2010, NHS Accessible Information Standard | A (Legal requirement) |
| Mental Capacity Assessment | Capacity assessments must be documented for relevant decisions when capacity is in question | Mental Capacity Act 2005, NICE NG108 | A (Legal requirement) |
| Medication Reconciliation | Complete medication history including allergies must be captured and maintained | NICE NG5, NHS Patient Safety Strategy | A (High evidence) |
| Care Planning | Patients with long-term conditions should have documented care plans including goals and preferences | NICE NG56, NHS Comprehensive Model for Personalised Care | B (Moderate evidence) |
| Safeguarding Risk Assessment | Safeguarding concerns must be identified, documented and appropriately shared | Care Act 2014, Children Act 2004 | A (Legal requirement) |
| End of Life Care Planning | Advance care planning discussions and decisions should be documented | NICE NG142, Ambitions for Palliative and End of Life Care | B (Moderate evidence) |
| Social Circumstances Assessment | Social factors affecting health should be systematically captured | NICE NG80, NHS Long Term Plan | B (Moderate evidence) |
| Family History Documentation | Relevant family medical history should be recorded for risk assessment | NICE CG164, UK NSC recommendations | B (Moderate evidence) |
| Immunization Recording | All vaccinations should be documented with complete details | UK Immunisation Schedule, NICE guidance | A (High evidence) |

## Context for Key Intervention Groups

### Person-Centered Information Capture

The standard emphasizes capturing information directly from patients about their preferences, communication needs, and personal circumstances. The "About Me" section represents a paradigm shift from purely clinical documentation to incorporating the patient's voice. This supports shared decision-making and ensures care delivery respects individual values and preferences.

### Legal and Regulatory Compliance

Several interventions directly support legal obligations. The reasonable adjustments framework ensures compliance with equality legislation, while mental capacity and safeguarding documentation meet statutory requirements. These elements must be implemented as mandatory fields with appropriate access controls and audit trails.

### Clinical Safety and Quality

The comprehensive capture of allergies, medications, and clinical alerts directly supports patient safety initiatives. By ensuring this information travels with the patient across care settings, the risk of adverse events is significantly reduced. The standard mandates structured recording using approved terminologies (dm+d for medications, SNOMED CT for clinical concepts) to minimize ambiguity.

### Integrated Care Delivery

Care planning sections enable multidisciplinary teams to coordinate interventions effectively. By documenting goals, actions, and responsible parties in a structured format, the standard supports the delivery of integrated care pathways. This is particularly important for patients with complex needs who receive support from multiple services.

### Population Health Management

Systematic capture of social determinants, lifestyle factors, and risk assessments enables population-level analysis and targeted interventions. The standard includes fields for occupation, housing status, and social circumstances that are often missing from traditional clinical systems but crucial for addressing health inequalities.

title: Generic Personas
content:
## Overview

The following personas represent the key users who will interact with systems implementing the Core Information Standard. Each persona has distinct needs, capabilities, and contexts of use that must be considered during system design and implementation.

## Persona Details Table

| Persona ID | Occupational Title | Description | Targeted | Key Competencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| P001 | General Practitioner | Primary care physician responsible for comprehensive patient care, chronic disease management, and coordination of specialist referrals | Yes | Clinical assessment, diagnosis, prescribing, care coordination, EHR navigation, clinical coding |
| P002 | Practice Nurse | Registered nurse working in primary care, conducting assessments, chronic disease reviews, and preventive care | Yes | Clinical assessment, care planning, patient education, basic prescribing, data entry |
| P003 | Community Nurse | Nurse providing care in patients' homes, managing complex conditions and coordinating with multiple services | Yes | Complex care management, wound care, medication administration, mobile technology use |
| P004 | Social Worker | Professional assessing social needs, arranging support services, and conducting safeguarding assessments | Yes | Social assessment, safeguarding procedures, multi-agency working, care navigation |
| P005 | Hospital Doctor | Secondary care physician managing acute episodes, conducting specialist assessments | Yes | Specialist clinical knowledge, acute care management, discharge planning, system integration |
| P006 | Allied Health Professional | Physiotherapist, occupational therapist, or other AHP providing specialized assessments and interventions | Yes | Profession-specific assessment, rehabilitation planning, outcome measurement |
| P007 | Care Coordinator | Professional responsible for coordinating care across multiple providers for complex patients | Yes | Care pathway knowledge, communication skills, system navigation, scheduling |
| P008 | Administrator | Non-clinical staff handling patient registration, appointment booking, and data quality | Yes | Data entry, customer service, system administration, information governance |
| P009 | Patient/Carer | Individual receiving care or their authorized representative accessing their information | No | Basic digital literacy, understanding of own health conditions, communication of preferences |
| P010 | Pharmacist | Medication expert conducting reviews, reconciliation, and providing advice | Yes | Medication knowledge, interaction checking, patient counseling, prescribing systems |

## Detailed Persona Descriptions

### P001 - General Practitioner

Dr. Sarah Chen is an experienced GP managing a patient list of 1,800 individuals. She conducts 30-40 consultations daily, balancing acute presentations with chronic disease management. She needs rapid access to comprehensive patient information during 10-minute appointments, including alerts, current medications, and recent hospital contacts. She is responsible for maintaining accurate problem lists, conducting medication reviews, and ensuring coding meets QOF requirements. Technology comfort is high, but she is frustrated by multiple logins and duplicate data entry.

### P002 - Practice Nurse

James Thompson is a practice nurse specializing in diabetes and respiratory care. He conducts structured reviews, administers vaccinations, and manages recall systems. He needs templates that guide comprehensive assessments while allowing free-text capture of patient concerns. He often identifies social issues affecting health and needs clear pathways for referral. He is confident with clinical systems but requires intuitive interfaces that don't slow patient interaction.

### P003 - Community Nurse

Maria Rodriguez leads a community nursing team covering a diverse urban area. She visits 8-10 patients daily, many with complex comorbidities and social challenges. She needs mobile access to care plans, medication lists, and contact details for other professionals. Network connectivity is variable, requiring offline capability. She coordinates with multiple agencies and needs to document detailed assessments while maintaining patient rapport.

### P004 - Social Worker

David Patel specializes in adult safeguarding and mental capacity assessments. He works across health and social care boundaries, often accessing multiple systems. He needs to document detailed assessments, record multi-agency discussions, and track complex legal statuses. Information governance is paramount, with careful control over information sharing. He requires systems that support narrative documentation while extracting structured data for reporting.

### P005 - Hospital Doctor

Dr. Amara Okafor is a medical registrar managing acute admissions. She sees 15-20 patients daily across wards and emergency departments. She needs immediate access to GP summaries, recent investigations, and advance care plans. Time pressure is intense, requiring efficient navigation and clear presentation of critical information. She must document thoroughly for legal and handover purposes while managing clinical priorities.

### P006 - Allied Health Professional

Tom Williams is a physiotherapist working across community and outpatient settings. He conducts functional assessments, develops rehabilitation plans, and measures outcomes. He needs to document structured assessments while capturing patient goals and preferences. Integration with broader care plans is essential, as is communication with referring clinicians. He uses profession-specific outcome measures requiring flexible data capture.

### P007 - Care Coordinator

Priya Sharma coordinates care for patients with severe mental illness across multiple providers. She maintains oversight of 60 complex cases, tracking appointments, medication compliance, and crisis plans. She needs dashboards showing patient status, upcoming reviews, and outstanding actions. She facilitates multi-disciplinary meetings requiring real-time information access and update capabilities.

### P008 - Administrator

Karen Foster manages patient registration and data quality for a large practice. She processes 20-30 registrations weekly, ensures demographic accuracy, and handles access requests. She needs clear workflows for data entry, validation tools to prevent errors, and reports highlighting data quality issues. She has limited clinical knowledge but deep understanding of information governance requirements.

### P009 - Patient/Carer

Robert and Margaret Wilson are in their 70s, managing multiple conditions with increasing digital engagement. They want access to their records, ability to update preferences, and clear information about their care plans. Digital confidence varies, requiring intuitive interfaces with good accessibility features. They value security but struggle with complex authentication processes.

### P010 - Pharmacist

Aisha Patel is a clinical pharmacist conducting medication reviews in primary care. She needs comprehensive medication histories, allergy information, and recent biochemistry results. She identifies optimization opportunities and medicines-related problems. She requires detailed documentation capabilities and integration with prescribing systems. She often identifies adherence issues requiring sensitive documentation.

title: User Scenarios
content:
## Scenario 1: New Patient Registration with Complex Needs

### Narrative

Mrs. Jamila Hassan, a 68-year-old woman with moderate dementia and type 2 diabetes, has recently moved to live with her daughter following her husband's death. She arrives at the GP practice for registration accompanied by her daughter Amina, who has lasting power of attorney for health and welfare. Mrs. Hassan speaks limited English and requires a Somali interpreter for healthcare consultations. She uses a wheelchair following a stroke two years ago and has a history of medication non-compliance.

### Key Data Elements

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Demographics | Name, DOB, ethnicity, preferred language (Somali), interpreter requirements |
| Contact Details | New address, daughter as primary contact, multiple phone numbers |
| Legal Status | LPA documentation, capacity assessment for medical decisions |
| Clinical Alerts | Dementia diagnosis, stroke history, diabetes, medication compliance issues |
| Reasonable Adjustments | Interpreter needs, wheelchair access, longer appointments, written materials in Somali |
| Social Context | Recent bereavement, living with family, cultural preferences |

### Personas Involved
- P008 (Administrator) - Initial registration and demographic data entry
- P001 (GP) - Clinical assessment and care planning
- P002 (Practice Nurse) - Diabetes review and care coordination
- P009 (Patient/Carer) - Amina providing information and consent

### Corresponding Business Process
- BP001: Patient Registration and Initial Assessment

## Scenario 2: Multi-Disciplinary Care Planning for Frequent Hospital Attender

### Narrative

Mr. Thomas Murphy, 55, has had six emergency admissions in the past year related to alcohol dependency, depression, and unstable housing. Following his latest admission for seizures, the hospital team identifies him for multi-disciplinary review. He has capacity but variable engagement with services. A case conference is arranged involving hospital staff, his GP, community mental health team, and housing support worker.

### Key Data Elements

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Problem List | Alcohol dependency, depression, epilepsy, homelessness risk |
| Service Contacts | Multiple teams involved, attendance patterns, key worker details |
| Risk Assessments | Self-neglect, vulnerability, seizure management |
| Care Plans | Crisis plan, alcohol reduction strategy, housing support |
| Medications | Anti-epileptics, antidepressants, vitamin supplementation, adherence issues |
| Social Circumstances | Unstable housing, limited family support, unemployment |

### Personas Involved
- P005 (Hospital Doctor) - Discharge planning and medical management
- P001 (GP) - Primary care coordination and prescription management
- P004 (Social Worker) - Housing assessment and vulnerability support
- P007 (Care Coordinator) - MDT facilitation and plan monitoring

### Corresponding Business Process
- BP003: Multi-Disciplinary Team Care Planning

## Scenario 3: End of Life Care Preferences Documentation

### Narrative

Mr. William Chen, 78, has advanced COPD and has been identified for palliative care following recent ICU admission. During a home visit, the community palliative care nurse discusses his preferences for future care. Mr. Chen expresses clear wishes about preferring comfort care at home, declining further intensive interventions, and wanting his son involved in all decisions. He has capacity and wants to formally document an advance decision to refuse treatment.

### Key Data Elements

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Prognosis | Estimated months of life, disease trajectory |
| Advance Care Planning | Preferred place of death (home), DNACPR decision, treatment preferences |
| Legal Documentation | ADRT for ventilation/ICU, capacity assessment recorded |
| Family Involvement | Son as primary contact, family awareness documented |
| Anticipatory Medications | Just-in-case box location, authorization details |
| Service Coordination | Hospice at home contact, GP awareness, out-of-hours handover |

### Personas Involved
- P003 (Community Nurse) - Palliative care assessment and preference documentation
- P001 (GP) - DNACPR completion and medication prescribing
- P009 (Patient/Carer) - Mr. Chen and son expressing and confirming wishes
- P010 (Pharmacist) - Anticipatory medication supply and counseling

### Corresponding Business Process
- BP005: Advance Care Planning Documentation

## Scenario 4: Safeguarding Concern Identification and Referral

### Narrative

During a routine diabetic foot check, Practice Nurse notices unexplained bruising on Mrs. Dorothy Parker, an 82-year-old with mild cognitive impairment. Mrs. Parker seems anxious and mentions her grandson has been "helping with finances" since her husband died. She appears unkempt, having previously taken pride in appearance, and mentions she "doesn't have money for the hairdresser anymore."

### Key Data Elements

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Safeguarding Indicators | Physical signs (bruising), behavioral changes, financial concerns |
| Capacity Assessment | Mild cognitive impairment but retains capacity for daily decisions |
| Risk Factors | Recent bereavement, social isolation, family member managing finances |
| Professional Actions | Photography of injuries, detailed documentation, GP discussion |
| Referral Information | Adult social services referral, information sharing decisions |
| Follow-up Plans | GP review appointment, social work assessment arranged |

### Personas Involved
- P002 (Practice Nurse) - Initial identification and escalation
- P001 (GP) - Clinical assessment and safeguarding referral
- P004 (Social Worker) - Safeguarding investigation and support planning
- P008 (Administrator) - Appointment coordination and referral processing

### Corresponding Business Process
- BP004: Safeguarding Identification and Referral

## Scenario 5: Transition from Pediatric to Adult Services

### Narrative

Alex Johnson, turning 18 next month, has cerebral palsy, learning disabilities, and epilepsy. They attend a special educational needs school and receive support from multiple pediatric services. Transition planning begins with gathering comprehensive information about current support, future goals, and adult service requirements. Alex uses assistive technology to communicate and wants to pursue supported employment.

### Key Data Elements

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Reasonable Adjustments | Communication aids, easy-read materials, supporter presence |
| Current Support | Education plan, therapy schedules, equipment provision |
| Medical Management | Seizure control, spasticity management, regular medications |
| Capacity & Consent | Supported decision-making needs, areas requiring best interests |
| Goals & Preferences | Employment aspirations, independence goals, social activities |
| Transition Plans | Adult neurology transfer, learning disability team referral |

### Personas Involved
- P006 (Allied Health Professional) - Therapy needs assessment and equipment
- P004 (Social Worker) - Adult services eligibility and support planning
- P007 (Care Coordinator) - Transition coordination across services
- P009 (Patient/Carer) - Alex and parents participating in planning

### Corresponding Business Process
- BP006: Care Transition Management

title: Business Processes & Workflows
content:
## BP001: Patient Registration and Initial Assessment
**Process ID**: BP001

### Objectives
To ensure comprehensive capture of essential patient information at the point of registration, establishing a complete baseline record that supports safe and effective care delivery while meeting legal and regulatory requirements.

### Process Elements

| Type | Element ID | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Start Event | BP001.1 | Patient presents for registration |
| User Task | BP001.2 | Verify patient identity and eligibility |
| User Task | BP001.3 | Capture demographic information |
| User Task | BP001.4 | Record contact preferences |
| Decision | BP001.5 | Reasonable adjustments required? |
| User Task | BP001.6 | Document reasonable adjustments |
| User Task | BP001.7 | Record GP practice details |
| System Task | BP001.8 | Validate NHS number |
| User Task | BP001.9 | Capture emergency contacts |
| Decision | BP001.10 | Capacity concerns identified? |
| User Task | BP001.11 | Conduct capacity assessment |
| User Task | BP001.12 | Document legal representatives |
| End Event | BP001.13 | Registration complete |

### Process Flow

| From | To | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| BP001.1 | BP001.2 | Always |
| BP001.2 | BP001.3 | Identity verified |
| BP001.3 | BP001.4 | Demographics captured |
| BP001.4 | BP001.5 | Contact details complete |
| BP001.5 | BP001.6 | Adjustments = Yes |
| BP001.5 | BP001.7 | Adjustments = No |
| BP001.6 | BP001.7 | Adjustments documented |
| BP001.7 | BP001.8 | GP details recorded |
| BP001.8 | BP001.9 | NHS number valid |
| BP001.9 | BP001.10 | Emergency contacts recorded |
| BP001.10 | BP001.11 | Capacity concerns = Yes |
| BP001.10 | BP001.13 | Capacity concerns = No |
| BP001.11 | BP001.12 | Assessment complete |
| BP001.12 | BP001.13 | Legal status documented |

## BP002: Medication Reconciliation and Allergy Documentation
**Process ID**: BP002

### Objectives
To establish accurate, complete medication and allergy records through systematic reconciliation processes, ensuring patient safety across care transitions and supporting informed prescribing decisions.

### Process Elements

| Type | Element ID | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Start Event | BP002.1 | Medication review triggered |
| User Task | BP002.2 | Access current medication lists |
| User Task | BP002.3 | Interview patient/carer |
| System Task | BP002.4 | Query external medication records |
| User Task | BP002.5 | Reconcile discrepancies |
| Decision | BP002.6 | New allergies identified? |
| User Task | BP002.7 | Document allergy details |
| System Task | BP002.8 | Update allergy alerts |
| User Task | BP002.9 | Verify medication details |
| Decision | BP002.10 | Changes required? |
| User Task | BP002.11 | Update medication records |
| System Task | BP002.12 | Generate reconciliation report |
| End Event | BP002.13 | Reconciliation complete |

### Process Flow

| From | To | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| BP002.1 | BP002.2 | Always |
| BP002.2 | BP002.3 | Lists accessed |
| BP002.3 | BP002.4 | Patient interviewed |
| BP002.4 | BP002.5 | External data retrieved |
| BP002.5 | BP002.6 | Discrepancies resolved |
| BP002.6 | BP002.7 | New allergies = Yes |
| BP002.6 | BP002.9 | New allergies = No |
| BP002.7 | BP002.8 | Allergies documented |
| BP002.8 | BP002.9 | Alerts updated |
| BP002.9 | BP002.10 | Medications verified |
| BP002.10 | BP002.11 | Changes = Yes |
| BP002.10 | BP002.12 | Changes = No |
| BP002.11 | BP002.12 | Records updated |
| BP002.12 | BP002.13 | Report generated |

## BP003: Multi-Disciplinary Team Care Planning
**Process ID**: BP003

### Objectives
To facilitate collaborative care planning across professional boundaries, ensuring coordinated interventions, clear accountability, and patient-centered goal setting through structured MDT processes.

### Process Elements

| Type | Element ID | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Start Event | BP003.1 | MDT referral received |
| User Task | BP003.2 | Schedule MDT meeting |
| System Task | BP003.3 | Notify team members |
| User Task | BP003.4 | Prepare case summary |
| User Task | BP003.5 | Conduct MDT meeting |
| User Task | BP003.6 | Document patient goals |
| User Task | BP003.7 | Agree intervention plan |
| User Task | BP003.8 | Assign responsibilities |
| Decision | BP003.9 | Patient consent obtained? |
| User Task | BP003.10 | Document consent/best interests |
| System Task | BP003.11 | Distribute care plan |
| User Task | BP003.12 | Schedule reviews |
| End Event | BP003.13 | Care plan activated |

### Process Flow

| From | To | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| BP003.1 | BP003.2 | Always |
| BP003.2 | BP003.3 | Meeting scheduled |
| BP003.3 | BP003.4 | Notifications sent |
| BP003.4 | BP003.5 | Summary prepared |
| BP003.5 | BP003.6 | Meeting conducted |
| BP003.6 | BP003.7 | Goals documented |
| BP003.7 | BP003.8 | Plan agreed |
| BP003.8 | BP003.9 | Responsibilities assigned |
| BP003.9 | BP003.10 | Consent = No/Unable |
| BP003.9 | BP003.11 | Consent = Yes |
| BP003.10 | BP003.11 | Legal basis documented |
| BP003.11 | BP003.12 | Plan distributed |
| BP003.12 | BP003.13 | Reviews scheduled |

## BP004: Safeguarding Identification and Referral
**Process ID**: BP004

### Objectives
To ensure timely identification, documentation, and appropriate escalation of safeguarding concerns while maintaining information governance requirements and supporting vulnerable individuals.

### Process Elements

| Type | Element ID | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Start Event | BP004.1 | Safeguarding concern identified |
| User Task | BP004.2 | Document observations |
| Decision | BP004.3 | Immediate risk? |
| User Task | BP004.4 | Take protective action |
| User Task | BP004.5 | Assess mental capacity |
| Decision | BP004.6 | Consent to share? |
| User Task | BP004.7 | Document consent decision |
| User Task | BP004.8 | Complete safeguarding referral |
| System Task | BP004.9 | Submit to authorities |
| User Task | BP004.10 | Inform relevant professionals |
| User Task | BP004.11 | Create safety plan |
| End Event | BP004.12 | Referral completed |

### Process Flow

| From | To | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| BP004.1 | BP004.2 | Always |
| BP004.2 | BP004.3 | Observations documented |
| BP004.3 | BP004.4 | Immediate risk = Yes |
| BP004.3 | BP004.5 | Immediate risk = No |
| BP004.4 | BP004.5 | Protection arranged |
| BP004.5 | BP004.6 | Capacity assessed |
| BP004.6 | BP004.7 | Always |
| BP004.7 | BP004.8 | Decision documented |
| BP004.8 | BP004.9 | Referral complete |
| BP004.9 | BP004.10 | Submitted successfully |
| BP004.10 | BP004.11 | Professionals informed |
| BP004.11 | BP004.12 | Safety plan created |

## BP005: Advance Care Planning Documentation
**Process ID**: BP005

### Objectives
To facilitate sensitive advance care planning discussions, ensure legally valid documentation of patient preferences, and enable appropriate sharing of end-of-life care decisions across care settings.

### Process Elements

| Type | Element ID | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Start Event | BP005.1 | ACP discussion initiated |
| User Task | BP005.2 | Assess patient readiness |
| User Task | BP005.3 | Explore values and preferences |
| User Task | BP005.4 | Discuss prognosis sensitively |
| User Task | BP005.5 | Document care preferences |
| Decision | BP005.6 | DNACPR appropriate? |
| User Task | BP005.7 | Complete DNACPR form |
| Decision | BP005.8 | ADRT requested? |
| User Task | BP005.9 | Support ADRT completion |
| User Task | BP005.10 | Identify proxy decision makers |
| System Task | BP005.11 | Flag records appropriately |
| User Task | BP005.12 | Share with relevant services |
| End Event | BP005.13 | ACP documented |

### Process Flow

| From | To | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| BP005.1 | BP005.2 | Always |
| BP005.2 | BP005.3 | Patient ready |
| BP005.3 | BP005.4 | Values explored |
| BP005.4 | BP005.5 | Prognosis discussed |
| BP005.5 | BP005.6 | Preferences documented |
| BP005.6 | BP005.7 | DNACPR = Yes |
| BP005.6 | BP005.8 | DNACPR = No |
| BP005.7 | BP005.8 | DNACPR complete |
| BP005.8 | BP005.9 | ADRT = Yes |
| BP005.8 | BP005.10 | ADRT = No |
| BP005.9 | BP005.10 | ADRT documented |
| BP005.10 | BP005.11 | Proxy identified |
| BP005.11 | BP005.12 | Records flagged |
| BP005.12 | BP005.13 | Information shared |

title: Core Data Elements
content:
## Overview

The Core Information Standard defines comprehensive data elements organized into logical groupings. Each element includes validation rules, terminology bindings, and implementation guidance to ensure consistent capture across systems.

## Person Demographics

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| DEM001 | Person First Name | The first name(s) of the person, including middle names | String | M | Max length: 35 chars | BP001 | NHS_DD: person_given_name |
| DEM002 | Person Family Name | The family name or surname of the person | String | M | Max length: 35 chars | BP001 | NHS_DD: person_family_name |
| DEM003 | Person Preferred Name | Name by which person wishes to be addressed | String | R | Max length: 70 chars | BP001 | NHS_DD: person_full_name |
| DEM004 | Title | Person's title (Mr, Mrs, Dr, etc.) | String | R | Max length: 35 chars | BP001 | NHS_DD: person_title |
| DEM005 | Date of Birth | The date of birth of the person | Date | M | Format: YYYY-MM-DD, Not future date | BP001 | NHS_DD: person_birth_date |
| DEM006 | Gender | Person's stated gender | String | R | Max length: 1 char, Values: M/F/X | BP001 | NHS_DD: person_stated_gender_code |
| DEM007 | Sex | Person's phenotypic sex for clinical purposes | String | O | Max length: 1 char, Values: M/F/X | BP001 | NHS_DD: person_phenotypic_sex |
| DEM008 | Ethnicity | Ethnicity as specified by the person | String | R | Max length: 2 chars, NHS DD codes | BP001 | NHS_DD: ethnic_category |
| DEM009 | Religion | Religious affiliation as specified by person | String | R | SNOMED CT subset | BP001 | SNOMED_CT: 999000531000000100 |
| DEM010 | NHS Number | Unique NHS identifier | String | R | 10 digits, Modulus 11 check | BP001 | NHS_DD: nhs_number |
| DEM011 | NHS Number Status | Reliability indicator for NHS number | String | R | Max length: 2 chars, Valid codes only | BP001 | NHS_DD: nhs_number_status_indicator |

## Contact Information

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CON001 | Address Line 1 | First line of patient address | String | M | Max length: 35 chars | BP001 | NHS_DD: patient_usual_address |
| CON002 | Address Line 2-5 | Additional address lines | String | R | Max length: 35 chars each | BP001 | NHS_DD: patient_usual_address |
| CON003 | Postcode | UK postcode | String | R | Valid UK postcode format | BP001 | NHS_DD: postcode_of_usual_address |
| CON004 | Address Type | Type of address (home, temporary, etc.) | String | R | Max length: 1 char, Valid codes | BP001 | NHS_DD: address_association_type |
| CON005 | Telephone Number | Contact telephone number | String | R | Max length: 35 chars | BP001 | NHS_DD: uk_telephone_number |
| CON006 | Telephone Type | Type of phone (home, mobile, work) | String | R | Max length: 6 chars, FHIR values | BP001 | FHIR: contact-point-use |
| CON007 | Email Address | Contact email address | String | R | Max length: 255, Valid email format | BP001 | NHS_DD: contact_email_address |
| CON008 | Preferred Contact Method | How person prefers to be contacted | String | R | Max length: 1 char, Valid codes | BP001 | NHS_DD: communication_contact_method |

## Clinical Information

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CLIN001 | Problem/Diagnosis | Active problem or diagnosis | String | M | SNOMED CT required | BP003 | SNOMED_CT: 1127581000000103 |
| CLIN002 | Onset Date | When problem started | Date | R | Not future date | BP003 | Generic date |
| CLIN003 | Problem Status | Active/Inactive/Resolved | String | R | Valid status codes | BP003 | FHIR: condition-clinical |
| CLIN004 | Allergy Substance | Causative agent for allergy | String | M | dm+d or SNOMED CT | BP002 | DM&D/SNOMED_CT |
| CLIN005 | Allergy Type | Allergy/Intolerance/Adverse | String | R | FHIR value set | BP002 | FHIR: allergy-intolerance-type |
| CLIN006 | Allergy Severity | Mild/Moderate/Severe | String | R | FHIR value set | BP002 | FHIR: reaction-event-severity |
| CLIN007 | Allergy Status | Confirmed/Suspected/Refuted | String | R | Valid status codes | BP002 | FHIR: allergy-verification-status |

## Medications

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| MED001 | Medication Name | Name of medication | String | M | dm+d preferred | BP002 | DM&D: VTM/VMP/AMP |
| MED002 | Dose | Dose amount | String | R | Numeric + units | BP002 | UCUM units |
| MED003 | Frequency | How often taken | String | R | Structured dose syntax | BP002 | FHIR dose syntax |
| MED004 | Route | Route of administration | String | R | SNOMED CT subset | BP002 | SNOMED_CT: 999000051000001100 |
| MED005 | Start Date | When medication started | Date | R | Not future date | BP002 | Generic date |
| MED006 | Review Date | Next review due | Date | R | Future date | BP002 | Generic date |
| MED007 | Prescriber Type | Who prescribed (GP, hospital, etc.) | String | R | Valid prescriber codes | BP002 | FHIR: medication-prescribing-agency |

## Reasonable Adjustments

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| ADJ001 | Adjustment Flag | Requires reasonable adjustments | Boolean | R | True/False | BP001 | SNOMED_CT: 1108111000000107 |
| ADJ002 | Adjustment Type | Type of adjustment needed | String | R | SNOMED CT subset | BP001 | SNOMED_CT: 999002121000000109 |
| ADJ003 | Adjustment Details | Specific requirements | Text | R | Max 500 chars | BP001 | Free text |
| ADJ004 | Communication Needs | Specific communication requirements | String | R | SNOMED CT subset | BP001 | SNOMED_CT: 999002151000000104 |
| ADJ005 | Information Format | Required information formats | String | R | SNOMED CT subset | BP001 | SNOMED_CT: 999002141000000102 |

## Mental Capacity and Legal Status

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| LEG001 | Capacity Status | Has capacity for healthcare decisions | String | R | Valid status codes | BP003, BP005 | SNOMED CT codes |
| LEG002 | LPA Flag | Has LPA for health and welfare | Boolean | R | True/False | BP005 | SNOMED_CT: 999001951000000107 |
| LEG003 | LPA Details | Name and contact of attorney | Text | R | Max 500 chars | BP005 | Free text |
| LEG004 | ADRT Flag | Has advance decision to refuse treatment | Boolean | R | True/False | BP005 | SNOMED_CT: 816281000000101 |
| LEG005 | ADRT Details | Specific refusals documented | Text | R | Structured format | BP005 | Free text |
| LEG006 | MHA Status | Mental Health Act status | String | R | NHS DD codes | BP003 | NHS_DD: MHA_legal_status |
| LEG007 | DoLS Status | Deprivation of Liberty status | String | R | Valid status codes | BP003 | Free text |

## Safeguarding

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SAFE001 | Safeguarding Flag | Active safeguarding concerns | Boolean | R | True/False | BP004 | Boolean |
| SAFE002 | Safeguarding Type | Type of safeguarding concern | String | R | SNOMED CT subset | BP004 | SNOMED_CT: 999002381000000108 |
| SAFE003 | Safeguarding Details | Specific concerns | Text | R | Max 1000 chars | BP004 | Free text |
| SAFE004 | Lead Agency | Safeguarding lead organization | String | R | ODS code | BP004 | NHS_DD: organisation_code |
| SAFE005 | Review Date | Next safeguarding review | Date | R | Future date | BP004 | Generic date |

## Care Planning

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CARE001 | Care Plan Type | Type of care plan | String | R | SNOMED CT subset | BP003 | SNOMED_CT: 999001471000000108 |
| CARE002 | Patient Goals | Patient's stated goals | Text | R | Max 500 chars | BP003 | Free text |
| CARE003 | Planned Actions | Specific interventions planned | Text | R | Structured format | BP003 | SNOMED_CT: procedures |
| CARE004 | Responsible Professional | Lead professional for action | String | R | Name and role | BP003 | Free text |
| CARE005 | Review Date | When plan should be reviewed | Date | R | Future date | BP003 | Generic date |
| CARE006 | Patient Agreement | Patient agreed to plan | Boolean | R | True/False | BP003 | Boolean |

## End of Life Care

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| EOL001 | On EoL Register | On palliative care register | Boolean | R | True/False | BP005 | Boolean |
| EOL002 | Preferred Place Death | Where person wants to die | String | R | Valid location codes | BP005 | Custom codes |
| EOL003 | DNACPR Status | Do not attempt CPR decision | Boolean | R | True/False | BP005 | SNOMED_CT: 450475007/450476008 |
| EOL004 | DNACPR Date | When decision made | Date | R | Not future date | BP005 | Generic date |
| EOL005 | Prognosis | Estimated prognosis | String | R | Days/Weeks/Months | BP005 | Free text |
| EOL006 | Awareness Status | Patient awareness of prognosis | String | R | Full/Partial/Unaware | BP005 | Free text |

title: Decision-Support Logic
content:
## Overview

The decision support logic embedded within the Core Information Standard ensures data quality, clinical safety, and appropriate care coordination. These decision tables define automated rules that trigger alerts, validations, and workflow guidance based on documented information.

## DT001: Reasonable Adjustments Alert Generation

### Description
Automatically identifies when reasonable adjustments should be flagged based on documented conditions, communication needs, or explicit requests. Ensures compliance with Equality Act 2010 requirements.

### Input Elements
- Diagnosis/Problem list entries
- Communication preferences
- Sensory impairments documented
- Learning disability flag
- Mobility requirements

### Decision Table

| Condition ID | Input Conditions | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|
| DT001.1 | Diagnosis contains "learning disability" OR "intellectual disability" | Set Adjustment Flag = TRUE, Prompt for specific needs | Legal requirement to identify |
| DT001.2 | Diagnosis contains "dementia" OR "cognitive impairment" | Set Adjustment Flag = TRUE, Add "longer appointments" | Cognitive support needed |
| DT001.3 | Preferred language ≠ English | Set Communication Need = "Interpreter required" | Ensure language specified |
| DT001.4 | Diagnosis contains "deaf" OR "hearing impairment" | Set Communication Need = "BSL interpreter" or "Hearing loop" | Check preference |
| DT001.5 | Diagnosis contains "blind" OR "visual impairment" | Set Information Format = "Large print" or "Audio" | Confirm format preference |
| DT001.6 | Mobility aid documented OR wheelchair user | Set Access Need = "Step-free access required" | Physical accessibility |
| DT001.7 | Autism spectrum condition documented | Set Adjustment Flag = TRUE, Add "Quiet environment" | Sensory considerations |

## DT002: Safeguarding Risk Escalation

### Description
Evaluates combinations of risk factors to determine when safeguarding alerts should be raised and which professionals should be notified.

### Input Elements
- Age of patient
- Mental capacity status
- Living situation
- Recent attendance patterns
- Documented injuries
- Financial concerns raised

### Decision Table

| Condition ID | Input Conditions | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|
| DT002.1 | Age < 18 AND unexplained injury documented | Raise child safeguarding alert, Notify designated lead | Mandatory reporting |
| DT002.2 | Lacks capacity AND financial concern raised | Adult safeguarding referral, Document in care plan | Vulnerable adult protection |
| DT002.3 | Multiple A&E attendances (>3 in 3 months) AND lives alone | Flag for MDT review, Consider vulnerability assessment | Pattern recognition |
| DT002.4 | Pressure sores AND care package in place | Notify care coordinator, Review care quality | Care quality concern |
| DT002.5 | Disclosure of abuse/neglect | Immediate safeguarding referral, Ensure safety plan | Direct disclosure |
| DT002.6 | Sudden behavior change AND carer stress documented | Welfare check required, Support for carer | Risk prevention |

## DT003: Medication Safety Checks

### Description
Performs automated safety checks on medication combinations, dosing, and patient-specific contraindications.

### Input Elements
- Current medication list
- Allergy records
- Renal function (if available)
- Age
- Documented conditions

### Decision Table

| Condition ID | Input Conditions | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|
| DT003.1 | New medication = documented allergy substance | Block prescribing, Alert prescriber | Prevent allergic reaction |
| DT003.2 | Age > 65 AND anticholinergic burden score > 3 | Warning: Increased falls risk, Review needed | Elderly safety |
| DT003.3 | Metformin prescribed AND eGFR < 45 | Alert: Dose adjustment needed | Renal impairment |
| DT003.4 | Warfarin + NSAID combination | High risk interaction alert | Bleeding risk |
| DT003.5 | >10 regular medications | Polypharmacy review recommended | Medication burden |
| DT003.6 | Opioid prescription AND no laxative | Suggest co-prescription of laxatives | Prevent constipation |

## DT004: Care Coordination Triggers

### Description
Identifies when multi-disciplinary team involvement or care coordination is required based on complexity indicators.

### Input Elements
- Number of active problems
- Number of services involved
- Hospital admissions in past year
- Social circumstances
- Care plan presence/age

### Decision Table

| Condition ID | Input Conditions | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|
| DT004.1 | Active problems > 5 AND no care plan | Prompt care planning discussion | Complex needs |
| DT004.2 | Hospital admissions > 2 in 6 months | Flag for discharge planning team | Frequent attender |
| DT004.3 | Mental health + physical health + social care needs | Suggest integrated care team referral | Multiple domains |
| DT004.4 | Care plan > 12 months old | Review reminder to care coordinator | Maintain currency |
| DT004.5 | Transition age (17-18) AND complex needs | Initiate transition planning | Pediatric to adult |
| DT004.6 | Palliative care + no advance care plan | Prompt ACP discussion | End of life planning |

## DT005: Mental Capacity Assessment Prompts

### Description
Provides decision support for when formal capacity assessments should be considered and documented.

### Input Elements
- Diagnosis indicating cognitive impairment
- Decision type required
- Previous capacity assessments
- Behavioral indicators

### Decision Table

| Condition ID | Input Conditions | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|
| DT005.1 | Dementia diagnosis AND treatment decision needed | Prompt capacity assessment for specific decision | Decision-specific |
| DT005.2 | Refusing essential treatment AND no recent assessment | Flag for urgent capacity review | Safety concern |
| DT005.3 | Request for ADRT AND cognitive impairment | Ensure capacity confirmed before proceeding | Legal requirement |
| DT005.4 | Fluctuating capacity documented | Note best time for assessment | Optimize assessment |
| DT005.5 | Family disagreement about care | Formal best interests meeting required | Conflict resolution |
| DT005.6 | Financial decision AND cognitive concerns | Consider Court of Protection referral | Financial protection |

## DT006: End of Life Care Cascade

### Description
Ensures comprehensive end of life planning is initiated when indicators suggest patient is in last year of life.

### Input Elements
- Diagnosis with poor prognosis
- Surprise question response
- Performance status
- Expressed wishes
- Current documentation

### Decision Table

| Condition ID | Input Conditions | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|
| DT006.1 | "Would not be surprised if died within year" = Yes | Add to palliative register, Initiate ACP | Early identification |
| DT006.2 | On palliative register AND no preferred place of death | Prompt preferences discussion | Patient choice |
| DT006.3 | DNACPR in place AND family not aware | Flag for sensitive communication | Family involvement |
| DT006.4 | Preferred place = home AND no anticipatory meds | Arrange Just in Case box | Symptom control |
| DT006.5 | Prognosis < 3 months AND no welfare LPA | Discuss proxy decisions urgently | Time-critical |
| DT006.6 | Multiple hospital admissions AND palliative | Consider hospice referral | Quality of life |

title: Indicators & Performance Metrics
content:
## Overview

These indicators measure the successful implementation and ongoing performance of the Core Information Standard. They support quality improvement, regulatory compliance, and demonstration of patient safety outcomes.

## Core Implementation Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IND001 | Patient Record Completeness | Count of patients with all mandatory fields completed | Total registered patients | By practice, age band, ethnicity | Monthly | EHR audit |
| IND002 | NHS Number Recording Rate | Patients with verified NHS number | Total registered patients | By registration type | Weekly | Demographics module |
| IND003 | Reasonable Adjustments Documentation | Patients with disabilities having adjustments recorded | Patients with documented disabilities | By adjustment type, practice | Quarterly | Equality monitoring |
| IND004 | Contact Information Currency | Patients with contact details updated in past 2 years | Total registered patients | By age band, practice | Monthly | Data quality report |
| IND005 | Preferred Name Usage | Appointments using patient's preferred name | Total appointments where preferred name differs | By service, staff group | Monthly | Appointment system |

## Clinical Safety Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IND006 | Allergy Documentation Rate | Patients with allergy status recorded (including NKDA) | Total registered patients | By age, risk group | Weekly | Clinical system |
| IND007 | Medication Reconciliation Timeliness | Admissions with reconciliation within 24 hours | Total admissions | By ward, day of week | Daily | Pharmacy system |
| IND008 | High-Risk Drug Monitoring | Patients on high-risk drugs with monitoring current | Patients prescribed high-risk drugs | By drug type, practice | Monthly | Prescribing data |
| IND009 | Polypharmacy Review Rate | Patients on 10+ meds with review in past year | Patients prescribed 10+ medications | By age band | Quarterly | Medication reviews |
| IND010 | Allergy Alert Response | Allergy alerts acknowledged appropriately | Total allergy alerts triggered | By prescriber type | Monthly | Prescribing system |

## Care Coordination Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IND011 | Care Plan Coverage | Complex patients with active care plan | Patients meeting complexity criteria | By condition, service | Monthly | Care planning module |
| IND012 | MDT Documentation | MDT meetings with all attendees recorded | Total MDT meetings | By team, patient group | Weekly | MDT system |
| IND013 | Care Plan Review Compliance | Care plans reviewed within specified timeframe | Total active care plans | By care type, team | Monthly | Review tracker |
| IND014 | Goal Achievement Rate | Patient goals marked as achieved/partially achieved | Total goals set | By goal type, timeframe | Quarterly | Care plan reviews |
| IND015 | Professional Contact Accuracy | Professional contacts confirmed accurate at review | Total professional contacts listed | By role type | Quarterly | Data validation |

## Legal Compliance Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IND016 | Mental Capacity Documentation | Relevant decisions with capacity assessment recorded | Decisions requiring capacity assessment | By decision type, service | Monthly | Legal status module |
| IND017 | LPA Recording Rate | Patients with LPA details verified and recorded | Patients known to have LPA | By LPA type | Monthly | Legal documentation |
| IND018 | Safeguarding Referral Timeliness | Safeguarding referrals made within 24 hours | Total safeguarding concerns identified | By concern type, referrer | Weekly | Safeguarding log |
| IND019 | Information Sharing Consent | Patients with documented sharing preferences | Total registered patients | By age, capacity status | Quarterly | Consent module |
| IND020 | DNACPR Communication | DNACPR decisions discussed with patient/family | Total DNACPR decisions | By setting, clinician | Monthly | EoL documentation |

## Patient Experience Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IND021 | About Me Completion | Patients with About Me section completed | Eligible patients offered | By age, service | Quarterly | Patient portal |
| IND022 | Communication Needs Met | Appointments where communication needs were met | Appointments for patients with communication needs | By need type | Monthly | Patient feedback |
| IND023 | Care Plan Agreement | Care plans explicitly agreed with patient | Total care plans created | By capacity status | Monthly | Care planning audit |
| IND024 | Preferred Place of Death Achieved | Deaths occurring in preferred place | Deaths where preference recorded | By place type | Quarterly | Mortality audit |
| IND025 | Patient Portal Adoption | Patients actively using portal features | Patients offered portal access | By age, digital skill | Monthly | Portal analytics |

## Equity and Access Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IND026 | Interpreter Service Usage | Appointments with interpreter when needed | Appointments for non-English speakers | By language, service | Monthly | Booking system |
| IND027 | Accessible Information Provision | Information provided in requested format | Requests for alternative formats | By format type | Monthly | Communications log |
| IND028 | Digital Inclusion Support | Patients receiving digital access support | Patients identified as needing support | By support type, age | Quarterly | Digital inclusion program |
| IND029 | Home Visit Provision | Home visits provided when clinically needed | Requests for home visits | By reason, outcome | Monthly | Visit log |
| IND030 | Transport Support Access | Patients receiving transport support | Patients with documented transport needs | By appointment type | Monthly | Transport service |

## System Performance Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IND031 | Record Access Speed | Records accessed within 3 seconds | Total record access attempts | By time of day, location | Daily | System logs |
| IND032 | Cross-System Data Availability | Data elements successfully shared | Data elements marked for sharing | By system pair, data type | Weekly | Integration monitor |
| IND033 | Terminology Mapping Success | Terms successfully mapped to standard | Total terms requiring mapping | By source system | Monthly | Terminology service |
| IND034 | Alert Fatigue Rate | Alerts dismissed without action | Total alerts presented | By alert type, user role | Weekly | Alert log |
| IND035 | Data Quality Score | Records meeting all quality criteria | Total records assessed | By section, practice | Monthly | Quality dashboard |

title: Functional & Non-Functional Requirements
content:
## Functional Requirements

### Core Patient Information Management

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FR001 | Patient Registration | System shall capture all mandatory demographic fields with real-time validation including NHS number verification, postcode lookup, and duplicate checking | BP001 | Critical |
| FR002 | Multi-format Name Support | System shall support multiple name formats including preferred names, previous names, and cultural naming conventions with appropriate display logic | BP001 | High |
| FR003 | Contact Preference Management | System shall record multiple contact methods with preferences, restrictions (do not call times), and requirement for third-party contact | BP001 | High |
| FR004 | Relationship Mapping | System shall maintain relationships between patients, carers, and professionals with role-based permissions and contact authorities | BP001, BP003 | Critical |
| FR005 | Historical Data Tracking | System shall maintain full audit trail of changes to core demographics with reason for change and authorization | All | Critical |

### Clinical Information Capture

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FR006 | Problem List Management | System shall maintain structured problem list with SNOMED CT coding, onset dates, resolution tracking, and significance flagging | BP003 | Critical |
| FR007 | Allergy and Intolerance Recording | System shall enforce allergy documentation with severity, certainty, manifestation details, and automatic alert generation | BP002 | Critical |
| FR008 | Medication Reconciliation | System shall support multi-source medication reconciliation with discrepancy identification and resolution tracking | BP002 | Critical |
| FR009 | Immunization Tracking | System shall record complete immunization history with batch numbers, sites, reactions, and contraindications | BP001 | High |
| FR010 | Clinical Document Management | System shall store and index clinical documents with metadata, version control, and cross-references to care episodes | All | High |

### Decision Support and Alerts

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FR011 | Reasonable Adjustment Alerts | System shall automatically identify need for reasonable adjustments based on conditions and generate appropriate alerts | BP001 | Critical |
| FR012 | Medication Safety Checks | System shall perform real-time drug interaction checking, allergy cross-checking, and dose range validation | BP002 | Critical |
| FR013 | Safeguarding Indicators | System shall identify safeguarding risk patterns and prompt appropriate assessment and referral workflows | BP004 | Critical |
| FR014 | Capacity Assessment Prompts | System shall prompt for capacity assessment when relevant decisions are being made for at-risk patients | BP003, BP005 | High |
| FR015 | Care Coordination Triggers | System shall identify patients requiring MDT coordination based on complexity algorithms | BP003 | High |

### Care Planning and Coordination

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FR016 | Integrated Care Planning | System shall support creation of multi-professional care plans with goals, actions, responsibilities, and timelines | BP003 | Critical |
| FR017 | Goal Setting and Tracking | System shall enable SMART goal documentation with patient priorities, progress tracking, and outcome measurement | BP003 | High |
| FR018 | Team Communication | System shall facilitate secure messaging between care team members with task assignment and escalation | BP003 | High |
| FR019 | Review Scheduling | System shall automatically schedule and remind for care plan reviews based on condition-specific intervals | BP003 | Medium |
| FR020 | Outcome Reporting | System shall generate outcome reports showing goal achievement, intervention effectiveness, and quality metrics | BP003 | Medium |

### Legal and Regulatory Compliance

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FR021 | Consent Management | System shall record granular consent decisions for information sharing with full audit trail | All | Critical |
| FR022 | Mental Capacity Documentation | System shall enforce structured capacity assessment documentation with decision-specific recording | BP003, BP005 | Critical |
| FR023 | Legal Status Tracking | System shall maintain current legal status (MHA, DoLS, LPA) with automatic expiry alerts | BP003 | Critical |
| FR024 | Safeguarding Workflow | System shall support complete safeguarding workflow from concern identification through referral tracking | BP004 | Critical |
| FR025 | Advance Decision Recording | System shall securely store advance decisions with verification status and applicability conditions | BP005 | High |

### End of Life Care

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FR026 | EoL Register Management | System shall maintain palliative care register with automated inclusion criteria and review triggers | BP005 | High |
| FR027 | DNACPR Documentation | System shall enforce complete DNACPR documentation with mandatory review dates and communication tracking | BP005 | Critical |
| FR028 | Preference Recording | System shall capture detailed preferences for place of death, place of care, and treatment limitations | BP005 | High |
| FR029 | Anticipatory Prescribing | System shall support anticipatory medication prescribing with location tracking and access instructions | BP005 | High |
| FR030 | Bereavement Support | System shall identify and track bereavement support needs for families after patient death | BP005 | Medium |

### Patient Engagement

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FR031 | Patient Portal Access | System shall provide secure patient portal for viewing records, updating preferences, and communication | All | High |
| FR032 | About Me Functionality | System shall enable patients to document personal preferences, values, and care preferences | BP001 | High |
| FR033 | Proxy Access Management | System shall support delegated access for carers and representatives with appropriate restrictions | All | High |
| FR034 | Accessible Information | System shall generate information in multiple formats (large print, easy read, audio) on demand | BP001 | Critical |
| FR035 | Appointment Preferences | System shall honor booking preferences including time, location, and professional preferences | BP001 | Medium |

## Non-Functional Requirements

### Performance Requirements

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR001 | Response Time | System shall display patient records within 3 seconds of request under normal load conditions | Performance | Critical |
| NFR002 | Concurrent Users | System shall support minimum 500 concurrent users per deployment without degradation | Performance | Critical |
| NFR003 | Batch Processing | System shall process bulk updates (e.g., QOF extracts) outside core hours within 4-hour window | Performance | High |
| NFR004 | Search Performance | System shall return search results for patients within 1 second for up to 1 million records | Performance | High |
| NFR005 | Report Generation | System shall generate standard reports within 30 seconds, complex analytics within 5 minutes | Performance | Medium |

### Reliability and Availability

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR006 | System Availability | System shall maintain 99.5% availability during core hours (8am-8pm weekdays) | Reliability | Critical |
| NFR007 | Planned Downtime | System shall limit planned downtime to 4 hours monthly, outside core hours with 72-hour notice | Reliability | High |
| NFR008 | Disaster Recovery | System shall support full recovery within 4 hours (RTO) with maximum 1 hour data loss (RPO) | Reliability | Critical |
| NFR009 | Failover Capability | System shall automatically failover to secondary site within 5 minutes of primary failure | Reliability | Critical |
| NFR010 | Data Backup | System shall perform hourly incremental and daily full backups with offsite replication | Reliability | Critical |

### Security Requirements

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR011 | Authentication | System shall support NHS smartcard authentication and modern MFA methods (FIDO2, biometric) | Security | Critical |
| NFR012 | Role-Based Access | System shall enforce granular role-based access control with legitimate relationship verification | Security | Critical |
| NFR013 | Encryption | System shall encrypt all data at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3 minimum) | Security | Critical |
| NFR014 | Audit Logging | System shall log all access and changes with tamper-proof audit trail retained for 7 years | Security | Critical |
| NFR015 | Penetration Testing | System shall pass annual CREST-certified penetration testing with critical findings remediated within 30 days | Security | High |

### Usability Requirements

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR016 | Accessibility Compliance | System shall meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards for all user interfaces | Usability | Critical |
| NFR017 | Mobile Responsiveness | System shall provide full functionality on tablets and essential features on smartphones | Usability | High |
| NFR018 | Clinical Workflow Optimization | System shall require maximum 3 clicks to access frequently used functions | Usability | High |
| NFR019 | Training Requirement | System shall be usable by clinical staff with maximum 4 hours role-specific training | Usability | Medium |
| NFR020 | Error Prevention | System shall prevent data entry errors through validation, templates, and confirmation dialogs | Usability | High |

### Interoperability Requirements

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR021 | FHIR Compliance | System shall expose all data elements via FHIR R4 UK Core conformant APIs | Interoperability | Critical |
| NFR022 | NHS Spine Integration | System shall integrate with NHS Spine services (PDS, SCR, e-RS, EPS) using current standards | Interoperability | Critical |
| NFR023 | Local System Integration | System shall support HL7v2 and API integration with local PAS, labs, and radiology systems | Interoperability | High |
| NFR024 | Terminology Services | System shall integrate with NHS terminology service for real-time SNOMED CT validation | Interoperability | Critical |
| NFR025 | Document Exchange | System shall support IHE XDS.b profile for clinical document exchange | Interoperability | High |

### Scalability Requirements

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR026 | Patient Volume | System shall scale to support practices from 5,000 to 50,000 patients without architecture change | Scalability | Critical |
| NFR027 | Data Growth | System shall accommodate 20% annual data growth for 10 years without performance impact | Scalability | High |
| NFR028 | Geographic Distribution | System shall support multi-site deployment with edge caching for remote locations | Scalability | Medium |
| NFR029 | Service Expansion | System shall allow modular addition of new services without core system modification | Scalability | High |
| NFR030 | Load Balancing | System shall automatically distribute load across available resources | Scalability | High |

title: References
content:
## Standards and Specifications

1. **Professional Record Standards Body (PRSB)**. (2024). *Core Information Standard v2.0*. London: PRSB. Available at: https://theprsb.org/standards/coreinformationstandard/

2. **NHS Digital**. (2023). *NHS Data Dictionary*. Available at: https://datadictionary.nhs.uk/

3. **NHS Digital**. (2023). *Dictionary of Medicines and Devices (dm+d)*. Available at: https://www.nhsbsa.nhs.uk/pharmacies-gp-practices-and-appliance-contractors/dictionary-medicines-and-devices-dmd

4. **SNOMED International**. (2023). *SNOMED CT UK Edition*. Available at: https://termbrowser.nhs.uk/

5. **HL7 UK**. (2023). *UK Core Implementation Guide STU2*. Available at: https://simplifier.net/guide/uk-core-implementation-guide-stu2

## Legislation and Policy

6. **UK Government**. (2010). *Equality Act 2010*. Available at: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2010/15/contents
